hypothesis: On the Gongora et al. lattice-structure dataset with an initially biased training set, an active-learning loop that uses an ensemble of tensor-completion surrogates (NeAT + CPD-S) to estimate uncertainty and select new experiments will require at least 30% fewer physical evaluations to identify a design whose Young’s modulus is within 5% of the true optimum than an active-learning loop using a Gaussian Process surrogate with the same acquisition strategy.
